feed.title: SAP Community - PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance
feed.subtitle: PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Delay one operation of task in maintenance plan
entry.content:
Hi!

I created a task with multiple operations.

All operations have a weekly cycle, but one of them has a 6 week cycle.

Now I want to create multiple maintenance plans which reference this task; my problem is, if I launch them at the same time, every maintenance plan will bring up the 6 week cycle operation on the same week.

Right now I see one option: launch the plans with a week delay each, this way the 6 week cycle operation will have a week delay aswell (compared to the other maintenance plans)

But is there a way to delay the operation itself when launching the plan? This way I could launch all the plans at once, but that one operation will have a delay.

Thanks in advance, I hope my question is understandable

entry.title: unable to delete PR in service order
entry.content:
HI team service created PR external created then PO created , PO gr 101 done then GR 102 reversed then PR relevant operation deleted , stil that PR unbale to delete , even similar case available for other order operation that PR deleted , PR deletion option found grade out , 

here looking help delete PR any how , any possible way please suggest us

entry.title: Plant creation design with S4 and Ariba as Indirect Procurement
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

We are having an S4 Implementation and we are confused with the strategy of creating New plants in S4. We are a manufacturing company and have the below structure.
1) Real Plants where manufacturing happens
2) Corporate office Locations where only Indirect Purchasing happens
3) Corporate office Locations where we sell Products and do Indirect Purchasing

Do we need to create plants for our corporate offices for indirect, non-manufacturing purchasing, corporate salaries, etc?
In which scenarios we need to Create Plants in S4?

We will be using Ariba for Indirect Procurement. Any help is appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: Make F2173 - Add Time Confirmation - Personnel Number Mandatory
entry.content:
For the Fiori App F2173, When we click on Go with or with out any filter. We are getting list of work order.
Once we click any workorder line and click on time confirmation, we get pop up. In that we have personnel number which is not mandatory.

 To make this field mandatory I have tried few things 
1. UI adapt - SAP didn't give the option to Make the field mandatory  -  not working

2. BADIs - BADI EAM_ORDER_FIELD_CONTROL(Not triggering), Workorder confirm and others  like  are running on update task., So couldn't raise an error message. 2708958, 3515215 BADI also not helped - not working

3. CDS extension(C_ObjPgMaintOrderAndOperation) - This makes the main selection screen mandatory  - not working

Please help me about the approach

Un Saludo.

entry.title: Question about Functional Location and Equipment Hierarchy comparison and best practice
entry.content:
Hi everyone, I have some questions regarding FLOC–Equipment.

First of all, I’m working as an instrument engineer (user side), so I’m not very familiar with SAP best practices. However, I’d like to compare the structure in my current company with my previous one.

In my former company, the functional location–equipment hierarchy looked like this:
S-01-UTL-060-01
(S = Company; 01 = Plant; UTL = Utility area; 060 = Unit 600—e.g., Air Separation Unit; 01 = Air Compressor Area)

The equipment hierarchy assigned there was, for example:
Main Equipment: S-060-C01 (Air Compressor)
Sub-equipment: S-060-FT-002

Technically, 060-FT-002 is not part of the compressor itself (it is installed on the discharge pipe to its consumer). However, it is assigned there because it makes systematic review easier.

The reporting, planning, and overall structure feel very intuitive and logical to me.

In contrast, my current company’s structure is as follows:

FLOC:
HM-UMT-UAS-AG01
(HM = Company; UMT = Company unit; UAS = Plant; AG01 = Unit—e.g., Plant Air)

The equipment assigned here consists only of major mechanical equipment, with no sub-equipment. Additionally, there are separate functional locations:
HM-UMT-UAS-AG01-INST (for instrumentation)
HM-UMT-UAS-AG01-ELC (for electrical)

In my view, this is not intuitive, and we are not utilizing SAP as effectively as we could, or as I understand it should be used.

I am considering proposing a restructuring of the hierarchy to be more similar to my previous company.

My questions are:

  1. Is it common practice to separate functional locations and equipment based on discipline? If so, what are the reasons or advantages compared to a more integrated structure like the one in my previous company?
  2. Where should instruments be placed—as main equipment or as sub-equipment—even if, technically, they are not part of the main equipment, as in the example above?

 

 

Thanks,

Ben

entry.title: Deactivate MRS
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are currently having MRS configuration but we are not scheduling work orders through MRS. Can you please let me know steps to deactivate MRS configuration.

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Create Maintenance Request App AdaptUI Notif Type Change
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to activate the Notification Type field in the Create Maintenance Request app using Adapt UI. However, while I can enable this field in one project, in another project the field does not appear. Only the notification type text is shown. What could be the reason?

1. project; There is no configuration for notification type Y1 in this system, so the issue may be caused by this. However, shouldn’t it at least display the notification type option? 

2. project;

entry.title: How to trigger mail notification for Work Item ---"manage change order" Fiori app
entry.content:
Hi Team,

How to trigger mail notification when  work Item for one of the workflow in Change record type under "manage change order " Fiori app is  created & sent to "My Inbox" fiori app ??

entry.title: Skip Maintenace plan call alternative FM
entry.content:
Hi Community,

My customer was using standard task list due to this some of maintenance plans calls have no operation so due to this issue when we run RISTRA20 program plan was not scheduled due to No operation error, to avoid this issue we have developed Automatic skip program by using This FM MPLAN_SKIP_CALL. How this program will work is will Schedule Two step job first we will run RISTRA20 2nd step is Automatic Skip program will run once we got error in Ristra 20 step that particular call this automatic program will skip it was skipping successfully,

But the problem when we run this Automatic skip program, Next planned counter reading is not taken as counter confirmation reading, even though we have activated check box for completion requirement. But it is working fine while doing Manul skip in IP10. But when we use This automatic program skipping counter calculation is wrong.

Can you please suggest if you ever encounter this issue and how did you resolve 

Please find below snapshot for better understanding

 

Best Regards 

Subba Rayudu

entry.title: Settlement through report - RKO7KO8G
entry.content:

Jaya

Appreciate your help.

Thanks

entry.title: Track procurement milestones in Maintenance Backlog Overview App
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I want to follow the status of external procurements ( non stock components and services ) in Maintenance Backlog Overview App. I have customized below procurement nodes in SPRO under maintenance order settings:

- Activate Event Type Linkage for Procurement Milestones

- Define assignment rules for Procurement Milestones

I am still getting following message in Maintenance Backlog Overview : "Milestones not tracked for order". Could someone provide help please ? Maybe I need to customize something else, Feel free to share any ideas and thoughts.

Find attached screens of related nodes in SPRO and the related App.

Thank you !

entry.title: Issue in Tyre Equipment Installation, Plant Synchronization, and Time Segment Data in SAP S/4hPublic
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are facing multiple issues in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (EAM – Plant Maintenance) related to tyre management using serialized equipment.

🔎Business Scenario

  • Tyre is maintained as a stockable serialized material
  • Equipment is automatically created from serial number (Equipment Category: X)
  • Tyres are installed on a Superior Equipment (Vehicle – Equipment Category: M)

❗Issues Faced

  1. Plant Not Updating for Child Equipment
    • When we change the Superior Equipment (Vehicle) plant from 1100 to 1200, the child equipment (Tyre) plant remains 1100
    • Expectation: Child equipment should align or system should guide correct process
  1. Installation / Dismantle Date Not Updating
    • Installation and dismantle activities are performed
    • However:
      • Valid From / Valid To dates are not updating correctly
      • CDS views (like I_EquipmentTimeSegment) are not reflecting proper data

🎯Business Requirement

We want to achieve the following process:

  1. GRN of Tyre (Serialized Material)
    • Equipment should be auto-created
  2. Activate Equipment View
    • Equipment should be ready for installation
  3. Install Tyre on Vehicle
    • Proper installation date capture
  4. Dismantle Tyre
    • Proper dismantle date capture
  5. Tracking Requirement
    • System should track:
      • Tyre lifecycle
      • Installation history
      • Running (usage) of tyre on each vehicle
  6. Plant Transfer Scenario
    • When vehicle (superior equipment) moves plant:
      • Either child equipment should update
      • OR system should enforce correct process (stock transfer + reinstall)

❓Clarifications Required

  1. Is the current behavior (no plant sync between superior and child equipment) standard in Public Cloud?
  2. What is the recommended process for:
    • Moving serialized equipment across plants?
  3. Why are Valid From / To dates not updating during install/dismantle?
  4. Any configuration required to ensure: 
    • Proper update of I_EquipmentTimeSegment CDS View?
  5. Best practice to track tyre running per vehicle in Public Cloud?

 

Regards,
Mohit Mishra

entry.title: Maintenance Scheduling Board: people not showing up for Operation assignment
entry.content:
Hi all,

I can't assign Operation to people of a Team because they do not appear after clicking "Assign People" button:

 

I'm getting crazy becasue I have created a Maintenance Work Center, I have assigned some people to this Work Center, I have created a Team, all people have "Authorized" status, but I can't to understand how they do not appear.

Thank you so much for your help.

Alessandro

entry.title: User status A is not getting updated when notification is set NOPR when order is created
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a user status for notification A which should be automatically set once notification is set to NOPR. I have done the transaction control setting in OIBS against A as Set for business transaction Put notification in process.

1. It works fine when I manually set notification in process, then user status is updated as A

2. It does not work when notification is automatically generated from order. Notification gets system status NOPR but user status is not changed to A.

Can anyone assist on how user status A should also be updated in the second case?

Thanks

Shweta Gupta

entry.title: Tyre Tracking Requirement – Design Confirmation & Performance
entry.content:
Our company has a total of 5,000 large fleets and approximately 100,000 tyres consumed per year. The client’s requirement is to track each tyre, including its installation, dismantling, retreading, swapping from one equipment to another, and tyre position.


We are planning to meet this requirement by treating each tyre as a serialized equipment.


Is mapping this process considered a best practice?
Since we have created 100,000 tyres as serialized equipment, will this impact system performance in the public cloud? Should we raise this concern with DAP?
